title: crotalaria podocarpa subsp. belckii
intro: Crotalaria podocarpa subsp. belckii, also known as the Belck's rattle pod, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in flavonoids, saponins, and glycosides, which contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Crotalaria podocarpa subsp. belckii is prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various ailments, and its essential oils are also extracted for topical applications.
